burial.plot_notes: Congregational Cemetery Castleton Rutland County Vermont per FA

narrative_blocks.header: First two wives both died within two years
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Hooker married Ruth Parmelee in December 1779, and she died September 4, 1781 — less than two years later, shortly after bearing their son Thomas Gould; he then married Ruth Hickok in April 1783, and she died November 16, 1784, barely eighteen months after the wedding. His third wife, Mary 'Polly' Coleman, whom he married at Poultney, Vermont in January 1787, outlived him by twenty years, dying in 1858 at approximately ninety-six — she was the one who stayed.

military_service.notes: FA: officially registered as Captain and veteran of the Revolutionary War.

burial.plot_notes: Congregational Cemetery Castleton Rutland County Vermont per FA

His third wife, Mary 'Polly' Coleman, whom he married at Poultney, Vermont in January 1787, outlived him by twenty years, dying in 1858 at approximately ninety-six — she was the one who stayed.

sources.page: Project completion contact for Chesterfield Hooker line research

narrative_blocks.header: A Vermont doctor who carried his trade west
narrative_blocks.body: Born in Poultney, Vermont, he trained as a physician and settled his family on the western New York frontier at Murray, where he practiced for decades. He and his wife died within eleven months of each other in 1856.

narrative_blocks.header: He buried four of his nine children young
narrative_blocks.body: Four of his nine children died before twenty-two — two boys at eight, and three more between seventeen and twenty-two — a punishing toll even by the standards of a frontier century.

burial.plot_notes: Sandy Creek Cemetery Murray Orleans County New York per FA
Sandy Creek Cemetery, Murray, Orleans County NY (per Findagrave). Same as husband Thomas Gould.

narrative_blocks.body: Cullen died in 1819 at the age of eight.

cross_connections.evidence: Thomas Edwin's father Thomas Gould (H01270) and Laura's father Myron (H01272) were half-brothers via Capt. Thomas Hooker (H00491) by different mothers. Two of Thomas Edwin's sons each married a Shader-line descendant of Laura: Thomas Harvey (H03872) m. Laura's daughter Caroline E. Shader (X02269); William Henry (H03880) m. Laura's granddaughter Lydia M. Robinson. Two cousin marriages closed the loop between the Thomas Gould and Myron lines.
cross_connections.display_label: Their children's marriages closed two Hooker-cousin loops
Her daughter Caroline Shader married his son Thomas Harvey Hooker
cross_connections.notes: Cousin marriage documentation. H02424 (Thomas Edwin Hooker) and HD3260 (Laura Electa Hooker Shader) are uncle and niece — Laura's father Myron Hooker (H01272) was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. Their respective children H03872 Thomas Harvey Hooker (Thomas Edwin's son) and X02269 Caroline E. Shader (Laura's daughter) were third cousins who married Feb 12, 1863. Children of that marriage (H05030 Lucy and H05031 Alvah) inherit double-Hooker ancestry with descent_paths_to_thomas=2.

bio.photo_notes: FA photo of Ruth Hooker Perkins.

narrative_blocks.header: Born two weeks before her mother died
narrative_blocks.body: Ruth Hooker was born November 2, 1784 in Poultney, Vermont — fourteen days before her mother Ruth Hickok Hooker died on November 16, 1784, leaving the infant motherless before she was a month old. She was the sole child of Captain Thomas Hooker's second marriage, and her existence was the last thing her mother witnessed; she went on to marry Rev. Sylvester Perkins in October 1805 and raised at least seven children in New York and Illinois, dying at Cortland, Illinois in September 1864 at seventy-nine, eighty years after the mother she never knew.

burial.plot_notes: Mound Rest Cemetery Cortland DeKalb County Illinois per FA

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 1272: b. Jan 1788 Poultney VT (year only). WikiTree records exact date Jan 17, 1787 — one-year discrepancy. Going with EH year 1788 per Sam's rule that EH overrides WikiTree on dates, but using WikiTree's day=17.

death.note: Per WikiTree: d. Dec 25, 1858 Kendall NY age 71. EH 1272 says "He died 1859" — likely an EH error since WikiTree has more specific date.

narrative_blocks.header: Vermont-born, Christmas Day death in upstate New York
narrative_blocks.body: Myron Hooker was born January 17, 1788 in Poultney, Rutland County Vermont, the son of Captain Thomas Hooker by his third wife Mary Coleman. By the 1850 census he had migrated west to Kendall, Orleans County New York, where he died Christmas Day 1858 at age seventy. He was buried at Greenwood Cemetery Kendall, far from the Hooker-line Connecticut center.

narrative_blocks.header: Presumed daughter's line reconverged Hooker branches
narrative_blocks.body: Through Laura E. Hooker Shader — Myron's presumed daughter by first wife Sally Brooks — the line runs forward to Caroline E. Shader who married Thomas Harvey Hooker, descendant of Myron's half-brother Thomas Gould Hooker. That third-cousin marriage between two branches of Captain Thomas Hooker's descendants reconverged a line that had split four generations earlier. Their child Charles Theodore Hooker qualifies as double Hooker ancestry.

burial.plot_notes: Greenwood Cemetery, Kendall, Orleans County NY (per WikiTree). Cemetery not yet built as separate CEM entry.

bio.extra_names: EH 1272 spells 'Brokes' — likely OCR/typo error in EH printing. Standard spelling 'Brooks' per WikiTree.

death.note: Predeceased Myron — date unknown but before he married widow Betsy Bacon.

sources.page: Kendall, Orleans, NY: Miron Hooker M 62 VT; Betsey Hooker F 58 CT
Kendall NY: Myron Hooker 67 Head; Betsey Hooker 63 Wife

birth.historical_notes: EH 2436 says b.1839, but Myron's wife Sally Brooks would have been advanced age in 1839 (sons Myron Smith b.1820, Thomas Cullen b.1822). EH date may be in error; further research warranted.

death.note: EH 2436 says "She died 1839" — same year as her recorded birth. Likely either an EH error in birth year (perhaps 1819?), or she died very young. If died in infancy, would qualify for died_in_infancy tag. Source ambiguous.

marriages.notes: Per EH 2436: m. Lemuel Bacon. One child Myron Bacon (EH 3890) who died 1864. Note: if EH dates 1839-1839 for Betsey are correct, marriage would be impossible — EH may have her birth year wrong (perhaps 1819).

marriages.notes: Per EH 2438: 'm. Marie ___' — only first name documented in EH; surname blank. Three children listed: Charles, Betsey, Frank.

bio.extra_names: Not in EH 1272 — almost certainly an EH omission. Per WikiTree (which lists her as Betsy Bacon's daughter, likely confused with Sally Brooks who was the biological mother).
bio.bio_blurb: Vermont-born Hooker daughter who lived to ninety-three

birth.historical_notes: Per WikiTree: b.1818 Vermont — making her older than her EH-listed siblings Myron Smith (1820) and Thomas Cullen (1822). Likely Myron Hooker + Sally Brooks first child; EH 1272 omitted her, possibly because she had no Hooker-surname descendants to track.

parents.mother_research_notes: Per Findagrave: Betsey Bacon Hooker (X02278). EARLIER hypothesis was Sally Brooks (X02277) based on timing logic (Laura b.1818, Sally Brooks's children Myron Smith b.1820 and Thomas Cullen b.1822). Findagrave likely correct that Betsey is the BIOLOGICAL mother — meaning Betsey may have been Myron's first wife, OR Laura was Betsey's daughter from her earlier Bacon marriage who took Hooker surname after her mother remarried Myron. EH 1272 omitted Laura entirely. UNRESOLVED — going with Findagrave per primary memorial source.

narrative_blocks.header: She buried the genealogist’s mistake by outliving everyone
narrative_blocks.body: Born in frontier Vermont in 1818, she lived to ninety-three — long enough to see the railroad, the telegraph, the Civil War, and electric light reach the country towns of her girlhood. One of her daughters, Violetta, raised thirteen children of her own.

cross_connections.evidence: Laura's father Myron Hooker and Thomas Edwin's father Thomas Gould were half-brothers via Capt. Thomas Hooker (H00491). Two of Thomas Edwin's sons (Thomas Harvey + William Henry) each married into Laura's line — closing two cousin loops.
cross_connections.display_label: Two of his sons married into her descendants
His son Thomas Harvey Hooker married her daughter Caroline Shader

HD3260 (Laura Electa Hooker Shader) and H02424 (Thomas Edwin Hooker) are niece and uncle — Laura's father Myron Hooker (H01272) was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. Their respective children X02269 Caroline E. Shader (Laura's daughter) and H03872 Thomas Harvey Hooker (Thomas Edwin's son) were third cousins who married Feb 12, 1863.

burial.plot_notes: Beach Ridge Cemetery, Brockport Monroe County NY — Section 2 Lot 56 (unmarked grave)

narrative_blocks.header: Lived to ninety-five; outlived the Civil War
narrative_blocks.body: Betsey Hooker Preston was born February 13, 1792 in Poultney, Vermont and died in 1887 at approximately ninety-five — born eleven years before the Louisiana Purchase, dying the year Grover Cleveland's first term ended, her life spanning the entire arc of American continental expansion. She outlived her father Thomas Hooker by forty-nine years, saw her brother Chesterfield die in 1864 during the Civil War, and was likely the last survivor among Thomas Hooker's ten children.

naming_inspiration.description: Named for his maternal uncle Chesterfield Hooker, son of Thomas Hooker (EH #491) and Mary Coleman, born 1797 in Poultney, Vermont.

Three known: Nelson Luther (3897, d.1862 Civil War age 17), Sarah Louise (3898, d. age 1), Edward Hooker (*3899, b.1852).

marriages.notes: Per EH 2448 + Findagrave: m1. Sept 9, 1862 Sarah Parker Harris (1837-1868), daughter of Nathaniel Harris of Middlebury VT. She d. April 27, 1868 Middlebury VT age 30 (likely childbirth complications given timing of 3 children). Three children by 1st marriage: Edward Harris (*1863-1932), Frederick Nathaniel (1865-?), William David (1867-?).

Per EH 2448 + Wikipedia + Findagrave: m2. Sept 28, 1871 Julia Almira Ashley (1839-1915), daughter of Dr. William Bliss + Julia Cornwall (Hall) Ashley of Milwaukee WI, b. Nov 16, 1839 Glastonbury CT. Per Milwaukee historical record: Julia's father was rector of St. Paul's Episcopal Church Milwaukee. Three daughters: Alice Ashley (1875-1966 m. Frank McCutcheon), Julia Elizabeth (1877-1956), Edith Mary (1885-1975 m. Greenwood).

education.notes: Per Wikipedia: graduated Middlebury College 1853. EH 2448 doesn't specify.

careers.employer: Self-employed → Palmer and Hooker law firm
careers.notes: Per Wikipedia + Milwaukee historical record: admitted to bar 1856; same year moved to Milwaukee. Law firm Palmer and Hooker formed 1864 with Henry Palmer. Per period biography: 'a lawyer of note.'
Served three terms as City Attorney per Milwaukee historical record.

21st (some sources say 18th) Mayor of Milwaukee, elected 1872. Population then ~75,000. Mayor-bicameral council form. Predecessor + successor: Harrison Ludington. Major mayoral works: 13 miles of water pipe laid, pumping engines installed, water reservoir built. One-term.

Per Milwaukee historical record + Wikipedia: appointed General Counsel by Henry Palmer (his former law partner, who became NWML president). Held position until sudden death March 1888 from heart disease in Jacksonville FL.

sources.short: Findagrave: David Griswold Hooker (Forest Home Cemetery Milwaukee)
Wisconsin State Bar Association Report of proceedings

narrative_blocks.header: Vermont farm boy to Milwaukee mayor
narrative_blocks.body: David Griswold Hooker — born September 4, 1830 in Poultney VT (named after his mother's Griswold maiden name) — left the family farm for college, graduating Middlebury College in 1853. Admitted to the Wisconsin bar in 1856, he moved to Milwaukee that same year and built his law practice over 16 years. He served as Milwaukee City Attorney three times (first term 1867-1870), formed the law firm of Palmer and Hooker in 1864 with Henry Palmer, and was elected the 21st Mayor of Milwaukee in 1872 — a single one-year term during which he laid 13 miles of water pipe, installed pumping engines, and built the city's reservoir.

narrative_blocks.header: Married twice; lost first wife at 30
narrative_blocks.body: David's first marriage to Sarah Parker Harris of Middlebury VT in 1862 ended with her early death April 27, 1868 at age 30 — likely childbirth complications after the rapid arrival of their three sons (Edward Harris 1863, Frederick Nathaniel 1865, William David 1867). David remarried in 1871 to Julia Almira Ashley of Milwaukee, daughter of Dr. William Bliss Ashley + Julia Cornwall Hall, and rector of Milwaukee's St. Paul's Episcopal Church. Three daughters by Julia: Alice (m.

marriages.notes: Per EH 2449: m1. Nov 9, 1857 Annah Baxter, daughter of Luther + Oledine (Rowe) Baxter of Cornwall VT, b. June 7, 1834 Cornwall VT. d. Nov 8, 1869 Castleton VT (age 35). No documented children — EH 2449 says 'CHILDREN, HOOKER. All by second marriage.'

Per EH 2449 + WikiTree: m2. Sept 6, 1871 Elizabeth Mary Muzzy (1844 India - 1912 Dorchester MA, age 68). Daughter of Rev. Clarendon Fay Muzzy + Samantha Bowen Robbins Muzzy (missionaries in India where mother died); ADOPTED by uncle Prof. Rensselaer D.C. Robbins + wife Sarah (Stuart) Robbins → known by Robbins surname per EH. Six children: Bessie Robbins Hooker (1872-1960 age 88), Stuart Van Rensselaer Hooker (*1874-1948 age 74 — surgeon), Emily Griswold Hooker (1875-1936 age 61), Edward Clarendon Hooker (*1876-1938 age 62 — m. Ruth Fairchild), Mary Stuart Hooker (1878-1961 age 83), David Ashley Hooker (1879-1951 age 72).

education.notes: Per EH 2449: 'graduated from Middlebury College in 1855.'
Per EH 2449: 'and from Andover Theological Seminary in 1861.'

careers.notes: Per EH 2449: 'He preached a while in Massachusetts.'

Per EH 2449 (published 1909): 'is now President of Rollins College, Winter Park, Florida.' Predecessor: Charles Grandison Fairchild (2nd president 1893-1895/1898). Two Rollins College Presidents are connected to the Hooker tree: Edward Payson Hooker (this entry) and Charles G. Fairchild (X-easter_egg, future father-in-law of Edward Payson's son Edward Clarendon — they married in 1907).

notable.notable_blurb: President of Rollins College Winter Park

narrative_blocks.header: He rose from country pulpit to college president
narrative_blocks.body: A Middlebury graduate trained at Andover Seminary, Hooker served as a Congregational minister in Massachusetts before taking the presidency of Rollins College in Florida. He led the young Florida college around the turn of the century.

narrative_blocks.header: His son married a fellow president's daughter
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker's son Edward married Ruth Fairchild, daughter of another Rollins College president, joining two academic families. Another son, Stuart, became a surgeon who had studied at Rollins.

narrative_blocks.header: His second wife was born in India
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker's second wife, Elizabeth, had been born in India to missionary parents; her mother died there, and an uncle raised her. Their six children all came of this second marriage.

cross_connections.evidence: Charles G. Fairchild was 2nd President of Rollins College (1893-1895/1898). Edward Payson Hooker was a later Rollins President (per EH 1909). Their children married each other 1907 — Edward Clarendon Hooker (H03917) m. Ruth Fairchild — connecting two Rollins presidential lines by marriage.
cross_connections.display_label: Both Rollins College presidents; their children married 1907
Both descended from Rev. Thomas Hooker; married cousins

narrative_blocks.header: He married a woman who also descended
narrative_blocks.body: Franklin Hooker married Marion Eliza Wilder Drake — and she, like him, was a descendant of Rev. Thomas Hooker of Hartford, Connecticut, making their 1853 marriage a convergence of two separate branches of the Hooker family tree across seven generations. Franklin descended through Thomas Hooker's son Samuel, while Marion descended through a different branch; the two lines had diverged in the 17th century and rejoined in Cornwall, Vermont, in 1853.

burial.plot_notes: Ward Cemetery, Poultney, Rutland County, Vermont

migration.notes: Among the first settlers of Bethlehem Parish, Woodbury. One of the leading men of the town for many years.

narrative_blocks.header: Among the first settlers of Bethlehem Parish
narrative_blocks.body: Around 1720 he was among the first settlers of Bethlehem Parish in Woodbury, Connecticut. He was described as 'for many years one of the leading men of the town.'

Edward Hooker's record says he 'died suddenly' in February 1756, at age sixty-seven. The unexpected death cut short his role as one of the leading men of Bethlehem Parish — a settlement he had helped establish thirty-five years before.

bio.notes: Also spelled Curtiss in some records
